questions.questionHtml: A hollow tilted cylindrical vessel of negligible mass rests on a horizontal plane as known. The diameter of the base is a and the side of the cylinder makes an angle θ with the horizontal. Water is then slowly poured into the cylinder. The cylinder topples over when the water reaches a certain height h, given by-
questions.solutionText: Solution Let the base of the cylinder lie on the horizontal plane. The water filled up to a vertical height h takes the shape of an oblique cylinder. The center of mass of this water will be at the centroid of its volume. By symmetry, the centroid of an oblique cylinder lies at the midpoint of its central axis. The vertical height of the center of mass is h 2 . Since the side of the cylinder makes an angle θ with the horizontal, the horizontal shift of the central axis at a height y from the base is y cot ⁡ θ . Therefore, the horizontal displacement of the center of mass from the center of the base is: x c m = h 2 cot ⁡ θ The cylinder will topple when the vertical line passing through the center of mass falls outside the base. The critical condition for toppling is when the center of mass is directly above the lower edge of the tilted base. The distance from the center of the base to its edge is a 2 . Equating the horizontal displacement of the center of mass to this distance, we get: h 2 cot ⁡ θ = a 2 h cot ⁡ θ = a h = a tan ⁡ θ Answer: h = a tan ⁡ θ
questions.solution: Solution Let the base of the cylinder lie on the horizontal plane. The water filled up to a vertical height h takes the shape of an oblique cylinder. The center of mass of this water will be at the centroid of its volume. By symmetry, the centroid of an oblique cylinder lies at the midpoint of its central axis. The vertical height of the center of mass is h 2 . Since the side of the cylinder makes an angle θ with the horizontal, the horizontal shift of the central axis at a height y from the base is y cot ⁡ θ . Therefore, the horizontal displacement of the center of mass from the center of the base is: x c m = h 2 cot ⁡ θ The cylinder will topple when the vertical line passing through the center of mass falls outside the base. The critical condition for toppling is when the center of mass is directly above the lower edge of the tilted base. The distance from the center of the base to its edge is a 2 . Equating the horizontal displacement of the center of mass to this distance, we get: h 2 cot ⁡ θ = a 2 h cot ⁡ θ = a h = a tan ⁡ θ Answer: h = a tan ⁡ θ
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let the base of the cylinder lie on the horizontal plane. The water filled up to a vertical height h takes the shape of an oblique cylinder.

The center of mass of this water will be at the centroid of its volume. By symmetry, the centroid of an oblique cylinder lies at the midpoint of its central axis.

The vertical height of the center of mass is h2.

Since the side of the cylinder makes an angle θ with the horizontal, the horizontal shift of the central axis at a height y from the base is ycot⁡θ.

Therefore, the horizontal displacement of the center of mass from the center of the base is:
xcm=h2cot⁡θ

The cylinder will topple when the vertical line passing through the center of mass falls outside the base. The critical condition for toppling is when the center of mass is directly above the lower edge of the tilted base.

The distance from the center of the base to its edge is a2.

Equating the horizontal displacement of the center of mass to this distance, we get:
h2cot⁡θ=a2

hcot⁡θ=a

h=atan⁡θ

Answer: h=atan⁡θ

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, v d drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.questionText: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, v d drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.question: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, v d drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.prompt: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, v d drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.html: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, vd drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.questionHtml: If E denotes electric field in a uniform conductor, I corresponding current through it, vd drift velocity of electrons and P denotes thermal power produced in the conductor, then which of the following graph is incorrect
questions.solutionText: Solution The drift velocity v d of electrons in a conductor is given by v d = μ E , where μ is the mobility and E is the electric field. Thus, v d ∝ E , which means the graph of v d versus E is a straight line passing through the origin. This makes the first graph correct. The thermal power P produced in the conductor is given by P = V 2 R . Since V = E l , we have P = E 2 l 2 R . For a given conductor, l and R are constants, so P ∝ E 2 . This means the graph of P versus E is a parabola opening upwards, making the second graph correct. The current I is related to drift velocity by I = n e A v d . The thermal power can also be written as P = I 2 R . Since R is constant, P ∝ I 2 . This means the graph of P versus I is a parabola opening upwards, making the fourth graph correct. Substituting I ∝ v d into P ∝ I 2 , we get P ∝ v d 2 . The graph of P versus v d should be a parabola opening upwards, not a straight line. Therefore, the third graph showing a linear relationship between P and v d is incorrect. Answer:

Thus, v d ∝ E , which means the graph of v d versus E is a straight line passing through the origin. This makes the first graph correct. The thermal power P produced in the conductor is given by P = V 2 R . Since V = E l , we have P = E 2 l 2 R . For a given conductor, l and R are constants, so P ∝ E 2 . This means the graph of P versus E is a parabola opening upwards, making the second graph correct. The current I is related to drift velocity by I = n e A v d . The thermal power can also be written as P = I 2 R . Since R is constant, P ∝ I 2 . This means the graph of P versus I is a parabola opening upwards, making the fourth graph correct. Substituting I ∝ v d into P ∝ I 2 , we get P ∝ v d 2 . The graph of P versus v d should be a parabola opening upwards, not a straight line. questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The drift velocity vd of electrons in a conductor is given by vd=μE, where μ is the mobility and E is the electric field. Thus, vd∝E, which means the graph of vd versus E is a straight line passing through the origin. This makes the first graph correct.

The thermal power P produced in the conductor is given by P=V2R. Since V=El, we have P=E2l2R. For a given conductor, l and R are constants, so P∝E2. This means the graph of P versus E is a parabola opening upwards, making the second graph correct.

The current I is related to drift velocity by I=neAvd. The thermal power can also be written as P=I2R. Since R is constant, P∝I2. This means the graph of P versus I is a parabola opening upwards, making the fourth graph correct.

Substituting I∝vd into P∝I2, we get P∝vd2. The graph of P versus vd should be a parabola opening upwards, not a straight line. Therefore, the third graph showing a linear relationship between P and vd is incorrect.

Answer:

questions.text: Two identical point charges + q are fixed at the opposite ends of a diameter of a semicircle. A third point charge Q is placed on the semicircular arc such that the radius connecting it to the center makes an angle of 60 ∘ with the diameter. If the total electrostatic potential energy of this configuration is zero, find the value of Q .

questions.question: Two identical point charges + q are fixed at the opposite ends of a diameter of a semicircle. A third point charge Q is placed on the semicircular arc such that the radius connecting it to the center makes an angle of 60 ∘ with the diameter. If the total electrostatic potential energy of this configuration is zero, find the value of Q .
questions.prompt: Two identical point charges + q are fixed at the opposite ends of a diameter of a semicircle. A third point charge Q is placed on the semicircular arc such that the radius connecting it to the center makes an angle of 60 ∘ with the diameter. If the total electrostatic potential energy of this configuration is zero, find the value of Q .
questions.html: Two identical point charges +q are fixed at the opposite ends of a diameter of a semicircle. A third point charge Q is placed on the semicircular arc such that the radius connecting it to the center makes an angle of 60∘ with the diameter. If the total electrostatic potential energy of this configuration is zero, find the value of Q.
questions.questionHtml: Two identical point charges +q are fixed at the opposite ends of a diameter of a semicircle. A third point charge Q is placed on the semicircular arc such that the radius connecting it to the center makes an angle of 60∘ with the diameter. If the total electrostatic potential energy of this configuration is zero, find the value of Q.
questions.solutionText: Solution Let the radius of the semicircle be R and its center be O . The two charges + q are placed at the ends of the diameter, say A and B . The distance between them is A B = 2 R . The third charge Q is placed at a point C on the semicircle such that the radius O C makes an angle of 60 ∘ with the diameter. The distances of C from A and B can be found using the properties of triangles. In △ B O C , O B = R , O C = R , and ∠ B O C = 60 ∘ . Using the cosine rule: B C = R 2 + R 2 − 2 R 2 cos ⁡ 60 ∘ = R In △ A O C , O A = R , O C = R , and ∠ A O C = 180 ∘ − 60 ∘ = 120 ∘ . Using the cosine rule: A C = R 2 + R 2 − 2 R 2 cos ⁡ 120 ∘ = 3 R The total electrostatic potential energy of the system is the sum of the potential energies of all three pairs of charges: U = 1 4 π ϵ 0 ( q 2 A B + q Q B C + q Q A C ) Given that the total potential energy is zero, we have: q 2 2 R + q Q R + q Q 3 R = 0 Dividing the entire equation by q R : q 2 + Q ( 1 + 1 3 ) = 0 q 2 + Q ( 3 + 1 3 ) = 0 Q = − 3 q 2 ( 3 + 1 ) Answer: − 3 q 2 ( 3 + 1 )
questions.solution: Solution Let the radius of the semicircle be R and its center be O . The two charges + q are placed at the ends of the diameter, say A and B . The distance between them is A B = 2 R . The third charge Q is placed at a point C on the semicircle such that the radius O C makes an angle of 60 ∘ with the diameter. The distances of C from A and B can be found using the properties of triangles. In △ B O C , O B = R , O C = R , and ∠ B O C = 60 ∘ . Using the cosine rule: B C = R 2 + R 2 − 2 R 2 cos ⁡ 60 ∘ = R In △ A O C , O A = R , O C = R , and ∠ A O C = 180 ∘ − 60 ∘ = 120 ∘ . Using the cosine rule: A C = R 2 + R 2 − 2 R 2 cos ⁡ 120 ∘ = 3 R The total electrostatic potential energy of the system is the sum of the potential energies of all three pairs of charges: U = 1 4 π ϵ 0 ( q 2 A B + q Q B C + q Q A C ) Given that the total potential energy is zero, we have: q 2 2 R + q Q R + q Q 3 R = 0 Dividing the entire equation by q R : q 2 + Q ( 1 + 1 3 ) = 0 q 2 + Q ( 3 + 1 3 ) = 0 Q = − 3 q 2 ( 3 + 1 ) Answer: − 3 q 2 ( 3 + 1 )
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Let the radius of the semicircle be R and its center be O. The two charges +q are placed at the ends of the diameter, say A and B. The distance between them is AB=2R.

The third charge Q is placed at a point C on the semicircle such that the radius OC makes an angle of 60∘ with the diameter. The distances of C from A and B can be found using the properties of triangles.

In △BOC, OB=R, OC=R, and ∠BOC=60∘. Using the cosine rule:

BC=R2+R2−2R2cos⁡60∘=R

In △AOC, OA=R, OC=R, and ∠AOC=180∘−60∘=120∘. Using the cosine rule:

AC=R2+R2−2R2cos⁡120∘=3R

The total electrostatic potential energy of the system is the sum of the potential energies of all three pairs of charges:

U=14πϵ0(q2AB+qQBC+qQAC)

Given that the total potential energy is zero, we have:

q22R+qQR+qQ3R=0

Dividing the entire equation by qR:

q2+Q(1+13)=0

q2+Q(3+13)=0

Q=−3q2(3+1)

Answer: −3q2(3+1)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400 Ω , what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L ?
questions.questionText: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400 Ω , what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L ?
questions.question: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400 Ω , what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L ?
questions.prompt: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400 Ω , what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L ?
questions.html: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400Ω, what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance RL ?
questions.questionHtml: In a voltage regulator circuit, the unregulated DC input voltage is fixed at 24 V . The Zener diode used has a breakdown voltage of 12 V , a maximum power dissipation limit of 240 mW , and requires a minimum knee current of 5 mA to maintain regulation. If the series resistance in the circuit is 400Ω, what is the permissible safe range of the load resistance RL ?
questions.solutionText: Solution The voltage across the series resistor R s is given by: V R s = V i n − V z = 24 V − 12 V = 12 V The total current I s flowing through the series resistor is: I s = V R s R s = 12 400 = 0.03 A = 30 mA The current I s is the sum of the Zener current I z and the load current I L : I s = I z + I L ⇒ I L = I s − I z To maintain regulation, the minimum Zener current is I z , min = 5 mA . The maximum load current is: I L , max = I s − I z , min = 30 mA − 5 mA = 25 mA The minimum load resistance is: R L , min = V z I L , max = 12 25 × 10 − 3 = 480 Ω The maximum Zener current is determined by the maximum power dissipation: I z , max = P z , max V z = 240 mW 12 V = 20 mA The minimum load current is: I L , min = I s − I z , max = 30 mA − 20 mA = 10 mA The maximum load resistance is: R L , max = V z I L , min = 12 10 × 10 − 3 = 1200 Ω = 1.2 k Ω Therefore, the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L is 480 Ω to 1.2 k Ω . Answer: 480 Ω to 1.2 k Ω
questions.solution: Solution The voltage across the series resistor R s is given by: V R s = V i n − V z = 24 V − 12 V = 12 V The total current I s flowing through the series resistor is: I s = V R s R s = 12 400 = 0.03 A = 30 mA The current I s is the sum of the Zener current I z and the load current I L : I s = I z + I L ⇒ I L = I s − I z To maintain regulation, the minimum Zener current is I z , min = 5 mA . The maximum load current is: I L , max = I s − I z , min = 30 mA − 5 mA = 25 mA The minimum load resistance is: R L , min = V z I L , max = 12 25 × 10 − 3 = 480 Ω The maximum Zener current is determined by the maximum power dissipation: I z , max = P z , max V z = 240 mW 12 V = 20 mA The minimum load current is: I L , min = I s − I z , max = 30 mA − 20 mA = 10 mA The maximum load resistance is: R L , max = V z I L , min = 12 10 × 10 − 3 = 1200 Ω = 1.2 k Ω Therefore, the permissible safe range of the load resistance R L is 480 Ω to 1.2 k Ω . Answer: 480 Ω to 1.2 k Ω
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The voltage across the series resistor Rs is given by:

VRs=Vin−Vz=24 V−12 V=12 V

The total current Is flowing through the series resistor is:

Is=VRsRs=12400=0.03 A=30 mA

The current Is is the sum of the Zener current Iz and the load current IL:

Is=Iz+IL⇒IL=Is−Iz

To maintain regulation, the minimum Zener current is Iz,min=5 mA.
The maximum load current is:

IL,max=Is−Iz,min=30 mA−5 mA=25 mA

The minimum load resistance is:

RL,min=VzIL,max=1225×10−3=480 Ω

The maximum Zener current is determined by the maximum power dissipation:

Iz,max=Pz,maxVz=240 mW12 V=20 mA

The minimum load current is:

IL,min=Is−Iz,max=30 mA−20 mA=10 mA

The maximum load resistance is:

RL,max=VzIL,min=1210×10−3=1200 Ω=1.2 kΩ

Therefore, the permissible safe range of the load resistance RL is 480 Ω to 1.2 kΩ.

Answer: 480Ω to 1.2kΩ

questions.questionHtml: The figure shows a square current-carrying loop ABCD of side 2 m and current i=12 A. The magnetic moment M→ of the loop is
questions.solutionText: Solution The loop is a square of side a = 2 m . The current i = 1 2 A flows in the path A → B → C → D → A . The magnetic moment is given by M → = i A → , where A → is the area vector of the loop. From the figure, the side A B is along the y-axis, so A B → = 2 j ^ . Since A B C D is a square, the side A D is perpendicular to A B . Thus, A D lies in the x-z plane. The vector A D → makes an angle of 30 ∘ with the positive x-axis and has a positive z-component. A D → = 2 cos ⁡ ( 30 ∘ ) i ^ + 2 sin ⁡ ( 30 ∘ ) k ^ = 3 i ^ + k ^ The area vector A → is obtained using the right-hand rule for the current loop: A → = A B → × A D → A → = ( 2 j ^ ) × ( 3 i ^ + k ^ ) A → = 2 3 ( j ^ × i ^ ) + 2 ( j ^ × k ^ ) A → = − 2 3 k ^ + 2 i ^ = 2 i ^ − 2 3 k ^ m 2 The magnetic moment is: M → = i A → = 1 2 ( 2 i ^ − 2 3 k ^ ) = ( i ^ − 3 k ^ ) A-m 2 Answer: ( i ^ − 3 k ^ ) A-m 2

The magnetic moment is given by M → = i A → , where A → is the area vector of the loop. From the figure, the side A B is along the y-axis, so A B → = 2 j ^ . Since A B C D is a square, the side A D is perpendicular to A B . Thus, A D lies in the x-z plane. The vector A D → makes an angle of 30 ∘ with the positive x-axis and has a positive z-component. A D → = 2 cos ⁡ ( 30 ∘ ) i ^ + 2 sin ⁡ ( 30 ∘ ) k ^ = 3 i ^ + k ^ The area vector A → is obtained using the right-hand rule for the current loop: A → = A B → × A D → A → = ( 2 j ^ ) × ( 3 i ^ + k ^ ) A → = 2 3 ( j ^ × i ^ ) + 2 ( j ^ × k ^ ) A → = − 2 3 k ^ + 2 i ^ = 2 i ^ − 2 3 k ^ m 2 The magnetic moment is: M → = i A → = 1 2 ( 2 i ^ − 2 3 k ^ ) = ( i ^ − 3 k ^ ) A-m 2 Answer: ( i ^ − 3 k ^ ) A-m 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The loop is a square of side a=2 m.

The current i=12 A flows in the path A→B→C→D→A.

The magnetic moment is given by M→=iA→, where A→ is the area vector of the loop.

From the figure, the side AB is along the y-axis, so AB→=2j^.

Since ABCD is a square, the side AD is perpendicular to AB. Thus, AD lies in the x-z plane.

The vector AD→ makes an angle of 30∘ with the positive x-axis and has a positive z-component.

AD→=2cos⁡(30∘)i^+2sin⁡(30∘)k^=3i^+k^

The area vector A→ is obtained using the right-hand rule for the current loop:

A→=AB→×AD→

A→=(2j^)×(3i^+k^)

A→=23(j^×i^)+2(j^×k^)

A→=−23k^+2i^=2i^−23k^ m2

The magnetic moment is:

M→=iA→=12(2i^−23k^)=(i^−3k^) A-m2

Answer: (i^−3k^) A-m2

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A singly charged ion (charge = 1.6 × 10 − 19 C ) is executing uniform circular motion in a uniform magnetic field of 0.5 T . The equivalent magnetic dipole moment associated with its orbital motion is 2.56 × 10 − 16 A ⋅ m 2 . If an electric field of 10 5 V / m , applied perpendicular to the magnetic field, makes the ion move in an undeflected straight path, then the mass of the ion is:

The equivalent magnetic dipole moment associated with its orbital motion is 2.56×10−16 A⋅ m2. If an electric field of 105 V/m, applied perpendicular to the magnetic field, makes the ion move in an undeflected straight path, then the mass of the ion is:
questions.questionHtml: A singly charged ion (charge =1.6×10−19C ) is executing uniform circular motion in a uniform magnetic field of 0.5 T . The equivalent magnetic dipole moment associated with its orbital motion is 2.56×10−16 A⋅ m2. If an electric field of 105 V/m, applied perpendicular to the magnetic field, makes the ion move in an undeflected straight path, then the mass of the ion is:
questions.solutionText: Solution For an undeflected straight path in crossed electric and magnetic fields, the velocity of the ion is given by: v = E B = 10 5 0.5 = 2 × 10 5 m / s The magnetic dipole moment M associated with the orbital motion of the ion is: M = I A = ( q T ) ( π r 2 ) = ( q v 2 π r ) ( π r 2 ) = q v r 2 The radius of the circular path in the magnetic field is r = m v q B . Substituting this into the expression for M gives: M = q v 2 ( m v q B ) = m v 2 2 B Rearranging for the mass m of the ion: m = 2 M B v 2 Substituting the given values: m = 2 × 2.56 × 10 − 16 × 0.5 ( 2 × 10 5 ) 2 m = 2.56 × 10 − 16 4 × 10 10 = 0.64 × 10 − 26 kg = 6.4 × 10 − 27 kg Answer: 6.4 × 10 − 27 kg
questions.solution: Solution For an undeflected straight path in crossed electric and magnetic fields, the velocity of the ion is given by: v = E B = 10 5 0.5 = 2 × 10 5 m / s The magnetic dipole moment M associated with the orbital motion of the ion is: M = I A = ( q T ) ( π r 2 ) = ( q v 2 π r ) ( π r 2 ) = q v r 2 The radius of the circular path in the magnetic field is r = m v q B . Substituting this into the expression for M gives: M = q v 2 ( m v q B ) = m v 2 2 B Rearranging for the mass m of the ion: m = 2 M B v 2 Substituting the given values: m = 2 × 2.56 × 10 − 16 × 0.5 ( 2 × 10 5 ) 2 m = 2.56 × 10 − 16 4 × 10 10 = 0.64 × 10 − 26 kg = 6.4 × 10 − 27 kg Answer: 6.4 × 10 − 27 kg
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
For an undeflected straight path in crossed electric and magnetic fields, the velocity of the ion is given by:

v=EB=1050.5=2×105 m/s

The magnetic dipole moment M associated with the orbital motion of the ion is:

M=IA=(qT)(πr2)=(qv2πr)(πr2)=qvr2

The radius of the circular path in the magnetic field is r=mvqB. Substituting this into the expression for M gives:

M=qv2(mvqB)=mv22B

Rearranging for the mass m of the ion:

m=2MBv2

Substituting the given values:

m=2×2.56×10−16×0.5(2×105)2

m=2.56×10−164×1010=0.64×10−26 kg=6.4×10−27 kg

Answer: 6.4×10−27 kg

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22 ∘ C . During the day, the temperature rises to 42 ∘ C and the tube expands by 2 % . The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.questionText: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22 ∘ C . During the day, the temperature rises to 42 ∘ C and the tube expands by 2 % . The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.question: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22 ∘ C . During the day, the temperature rises to 42 ∘ C and the tube expands by 2 % . The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.prompt: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22 ∘ C . During the day, the temperature rises to 42 ∘ C and the tube expands by 2 % . The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.html: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22∘C. During the day, the temperature rises to 42∘C and the tube expands by 2%. The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.questionHtml: Air is pumped into an automobile tube up to a pressure of 200 kPa in the morning when the air temperature is 22∘C. During the day, the temperature rises to 42∘C and the tube expands by 2%. The pressure of the air in the tube at this temperature will be approximately
questions.solutionText: Solution Using the ideal gas equation, P 1 V 1 T 1 = P 2 V 2 T 2 Given P 1 = 200 kPa T 1 = 22 ∘ C = 22 + 273 = 295 K T 2 = 42 ∘ C = 42 + 273 = 315 K Since the tube expands by 2 % , V 2 = V 1 + 0.02 V 1 = 1.02 V 1 Substituting the values: P 2 = P 1 V 1 T 2 T 1 V 2 = 200 × V 1 × 315 295 × 1.02 V 1 P 2 = 63000 300.9 ≈ 209.37 kPa The approximate pressure of the air in the tube is 209 kPa . Answer: 209 kPa
questions.solution: Solution Using the ideal gas equation, P 1 V 1 T 1 = P 2 V 2 T 2 Given P 1 = 200 kPa T 1 = 22 ∘ C = 22 + 273 = 295 K T 2 = 42 ∘ C = 42 + 273 = 315 K Since the tube expands by 2 % , V 2 = V 1 + 0.02 V 1 = 1.02 V 1 Substituting the values: P 2 = P 1 V 1 T 2 T 1 V 2 = 200 × V 1 × 315 295 × 1.02 V 1 P 2 = 63000 300.9 ≈ 209.37 kPa The approximate pressure of the air in the tube is 209 kPa . Answer: 209 kPa
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Using the ideal gas equation, P1V1T1=P2V2T2

Given P1=200 kPa

T1=22∘C=22+273=295 K

T2=42∘C=42+273=315 K

Since the tube expands by 2%, V2=V1+0.02V1=1.02V1

Substituting the values:

P2=P1V1T2T1V2=200×V1×315295×1.02V1

P2=63000300.9≈209.37 kPa

The approximate pressure of the air in the tube is 209 kPa.

Answer: 209 kPa

questions.text: A brass rod of length 500 mm and diameter 3 mm is joined to a steel rod of same length and diameter at 50 ∘ C . If the coefficients of linear expansion of brass and steel are 2.5 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 and 1.25 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 , then change in length of the combined rod at 200 ∘ C is
questions.questionText: A brass rod of length 500 mm and diameter 3 mm is joined to a steel rod of same length and diameter at 50 ∘ C . If the coefficients of linear expansion of brass and steel are 2.5 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 and 1.25 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 , then change in length of the combined rod at 200 ∘ C is

questions.prompt: A brass rod of length 500 mm and diameter 3 mm is joined to a steel rod of same length and diameter at 50 ∘ C . If the coefficients of linear expansion of brass and steel are 2.5 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 and 1.25 × 10 − 5 ∘ C − 1 , then change in length of the combined rod at 200 ∘ C is
questions.html: A brass rod of length 500 mm and diameter 3 mm is joined to a steel rod of same length and diameter at 50∘C. If the coefficients of linear expansion of brass and steel are 2.5×10−5∘C−1 and 1.25×10−5∘C−1, then change in length of the combined rod at 200∘C is
questions.questionHtml: A brass rod of length 500 mm and diameter 3 mm is joined to a steel rod of same length and diameter at 50∘C. If the coefficients of linear expansion of brass and steel are 2.5×10−5∘C−1 and 1.25×10−5∘C−1, then change in length of the combined rod at 200∘C is
questions.solutionText: Solution The total change in length of the combined rod is the sum of the changes in length of the individual brass and steel rods. Δ L = Δ L b + Δ L s Δ L = L b α b Δ T + L s α s Δ T Given that the initial lengths are equal, L b = L s = L = 500 mm . The change in temperature is Δ T = 200 ∘ C − 50 ∘ C = 150 ∘ C . Substituting the given values: Δ L = L ( α b + α s ) Δ T Δ L = 500 × ( 2.5 × 10 − 5 + 1.25 × 10 − 5 ) × 150 Δ L = 500 × ( 3.75 × 10 − 5 ) × 150 Δ L = 75000 × 3.75 × 10 − 5 Δ L = 2.8125 mm Rounding off to one decimal place, we get Δ L ≈ 2.8 mm . Answer: 2.8 mm

Answer: 2.8 mm

Solution
The total change in length of the combined rod is the sum of the changes in length of the individual brass and steel rods.

ΔL=ΔLb+ΔLs

ΔL=LbαbΔT+LsαsΔT

Given that the initial lengths are equal, Lb=Ls=L=500 mm.

The change in temperature is ΔT=200∘C−50∘C=150∘C.

Substituting the given values:

ΔL=L(αb+αs)ΔT

ΔL=500×(2.5×10−5+1.25×10−5)×150

ΔL=500×(3.75×10−5)×150

ΔL=75000×3.75×10−5

ΔL=2.8125 mm

Rounding off to one decimal place, we get ΔL≈2.8 mm.

Answer: 2.8 mm

questions.text: A 20 mg solid mixture of Li 2 CO 3 and Na 2 CO 3 is heated strongly, releasing 2.4 mL of CO 2 gas at 300 K and 1 bar. If the molar volume of CO 2 is 24.0 L under these conditions, what is the mass percentage of Li 2 CO 3 in the mixture? [Molar mass of Li 2 CO 3 = 74 g / mol ]

questions.html:
A 20 mg solid mixture of Li2CO3 and Na2CO3 is heated strongly, releasing 2.4 mL of CO2 gas at 300 K and 1 bar. If the molar volume of CO2 is 24.0 L under these conditions, what is the mass percentage of Li2CO3 in the mixture?
[Molar mass of Li2CO3=74 g/mol ]
questions.questionHtml:
A 20 mg solid mixture of Li2CO3 and Na2CO3 is heated strongly, releasing 2.4 mL of CO2 gas at 300 K and 1 bar. If the molar volume of CO2 is 24.0 L under these conditions, what is the mass percentage of Li2CO3 in the mixture?
[Molar mass of Li2CO3=74 g/mol ]
questions.solutionText: Solution Alkali metal carbonates, except lithium carbonate, are stable to heat. Therefore, only Li 2 CO 3 decomposes on heating. Li 2 CO 3 → Li 2 O + CO 2 Na 2 CO 3 → No reaction Moles of CO 2 evolved = 2.4 × 10 − 3 24.0 = 10 − 4 mol From the stoichiometry of the reaction, 1 mole of Li 2 CO 3 produces 1 mole of CO 2 . Moles of Li 2 CO 3 in the mixture = 10 − 4 mol Mass of Li 2 CO 3 = 10 − 4 × 74 = 7.4 × 10 − 3 g = 7.4 mg Mass percentage of Li 2 CO 3 = 7.4 20 × 100 = 37.0 % Answer: 37.0
questions.solution: Solution Alkali metal carbonates, except lithium carbonate, are stable to heat. Therefore, only Li 2 CO 3 decomposes on heating. Li 2 CO 3 → Li 2 O + CO 2 Na 2 CO 3 → No reaction Moles of CO 2 evolved = 2.4 × 10 − 3 24.0 = 10 − 4 mol From the stoichiometry of the reaction, 1 mole of Li 2 CO 3 produces 1 mole of CO 2 . Moles of Li 2 CO 3 in the mixture = 10 − 4 mol Mass of Li 2 CO 3 = 10 − 4 × 74 = 7.4 × 10 − 3 g = 7.4 mg Mass percentage of Li 2 CO 3 = 7.4 20 × 100 = 37.0 % Answer: 37.0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Alkali metal carbonates, except lithium carbonate, are stable to heat. Therefore, only Li2CO3 decomposes on heating.

Li2CO3→Li2O+CO2

Na2CO3→No reaction

Moles of CO2 evolved = 2.4×10−324.0=10−4 mol

From the stoichiometry of the reaction, 1 mole of Li2CO3 produces 1 mole of CO2.

Moles of Li2CO3 in the mixture = 10−4 mol

Mass of Li2CO3 = 10−4×74=7.4×10−3 g = 7.4 mg

Mass percentage of Li2CO3 = 7.420×100=37.0%

Answer: 37.0

questions.text: Which of the following statements are correct according to Molecular Orbital Theory? (A) In the N 2 molecule, the σ 2 p z orbital is higher in energy than the π 2 p x orbital. (B) The O 2 molecule is paramagnetic with two unpaired electrons in its π ∗ orbitals. (C) The C 2 molecule possesses a double bond consisting of one σ and one π bond. (D) N 2 + is structurally more stable than N 2 − despite both having the same bond order. Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.questionText: Which of the following statements are correct according to Molecular Orbital Theory? (A) In the N 2 molecule, the σ 2 p z orbital is higher in energy than the π 2 p x orbital. (B) The O 2 molecule is paramagnetic with two unpaired electrons in its π ∗ orbitals. (C) The C 2 molecule possesses a double bond consisting of one σ and one π bond. questions.prompt: Which of the following statements are correct according to Molecular Orbital Theory? (A) In the N 2 molecule, the σ 2 p z orbital is higher in energy than the π 2 p x orbital. (B) The O 2 molecule is paramagnetic with two unpaired electrons in its π ∗ orbitals. (C) The C 2 molecule possesses a double bond consisting of one σ and one π bond. (D) N 2 + is structurally more stable than N 2 − despite both having the same bond order. Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.html:
Which of the following statements are correct according to Molecular Orbital Theory?
(A) In the N2 molecule, the σ2pz orbital is higher in energy than the π2px orbital.
(B) The O2 molecule is paramagnetic with two unpaired electrons in its π∗ orbitals.
(C) The C2 molecule possesses a double bond consisting of one σ and one π bond.
(D) N2+is structurally more stable than N2−despite both having the same bond order.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.questionHtml:
Which of the following statements are correct according to Molecular Orbital Theory?
(A) In the N2 molecule, the σ2pz orbital is higher in energy than the π2px orbital.
(B) The O2 molecule is paramagnetic with two unpaired electrons in its π∗ orbitals.
(C) The C2 molecule possesses a double bond consisting of one σ and one π bond.
(D) N2+is structurally more stable than N2−despite both having the same bond order.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement (A): For N 2 ( 14 electrons), the molecular orbital energy sequence is σ 1 s < σ 1 s ∗ < σ 2 s < σ 2 s ∗ < π 2 p x = π 2 p y < σ 2 p z < π 2 p x ∗ = π 2 p y ∗ < σ 2 p z ∗ . Thus, the σ 2 p z orbital is higher in energy than the π 2 p x orbital. Statement (A) is correct. Statement (B): For O 2 ( 16 electrons), the configuration is σ 1 s 2 σ 1 s ∗ 2 σ 2 s 2 σ 2 s ∗ 2 σ 2 p z 2 π 2 p x 2 = π 2 p y 2 π 2 p x ∗ 1 = π 2 p y ∗ 1 . It has two unpaired electrons in its π ∗ orbitals, making it paramagnetic. Statement (B) is correct. Statement (C): For C 2 ( 12 electrons), the configuration is σ 1 s 2 σ 1 s ∗ 2 σ 2 s 2 σ 2 s ∗ 2 π 2 p x 2 = π 2 p y 2 . The bond order is 8 − 4 2 = 2 . Since the highest occupied molecular orbitals are both π orbitals, the double bond consists of two π bonds and no σ bond. Statement (C) is incorrect. Statement (D): N 2 + ( 13 electrons) has a bond order of 2.5 with 4 electrons in antibonding orbitals. N 2 − ( 15 electrons) also has a bond order of 2.5 but has 5 electrons in antibonding orbitals. Since N 2 − has more electrons in destabilizing antibonding orbitals, N 2 + is structurally more stable. Statement (D) is correct. Therefore, statements (A), (B), and (D) are correct. Answer: (A), (B) and (D) only
questions.solution: Solution Statement (A): For N 2 ( 14 electrons), the molecular orbital energy sequence is σ 1 s < σ 1 s ∗ < σ 2 s < σ 2 s ∗ < π 2 p x = π 2 p y < σ 2 p z < π 2 p x ∗ = π 2 p y ∗ < σ 2 p z ∗ . Thus, the σ 2 p z orbital is higher in energy than the π 2 p x orbital. Statement (A) is correct. Statement (B): For O 2 ( 16 electrons), the configuration is σ 1 s 2 σ 1 s ∗ 2 σ 2 s 2 σ 2 s ∗ 2 σ 2 p z 2 π 2 p x 2 = π 2 p y 2 π 2 p x ∗ 1 = π 2 p y ∗ 1 . It has two unpaired electrons in its π ∗ orbitals, making it paramagnetic. Statement (B) is correct. Statement (C): For C 2 ( 12 electrons), the configuration is σ 1 s 2 σ 1 s ∗ 2 σ 2 s 2 σ 2 s ∗ 2 π 2 p x 2 = π 2 p y 2 . The bond order is 8 − 4 2 = 2 . Since the highest occupied molecular orbitals are both π orbitals, the double bond consists of two π bonds and no σ bond. Statement (C) is incorrect. Statement (D): N 2 + ( 13 electrons) has a bond order of 2.5 with 4 electrons in antibonding orbitals. N 2 − ( 15 electrons) also has a bond order of 2.5 but has 5 electrons in antibonding orbitals. Since N 2 − has more electrons in destabilizing antibonding orbitals, N 2 + is structurally more stable. Statement (D) is correct. Therefore, statements (A), (B), and (D) are correct. Answer: (A), (B) and (D) only
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Statement (A): For N2 (14 electrons), the molecular orbital energy sequence is σ1s<σ1s∗<σ2s<σ2s∗<π2px=π2py<σ2pz<π2px∗=π2py∗<σ2pz∗. Thus, the σ2pz orbital is higher in energy than the π2px orbital. Statement (A) is correct.

Statement (B): For O2 (16 electrons), the configuration is σ1s2σ1s∗2σ2s2σ2s∗2σ2pz2π2px2=π2py2π2px∗1=π2py∗1. It has two unpaired electrons in its π∗ orbitals, making it paramagnetic. Statement (B) is correct.

Statement (C): For C2 (12 electrons), the configuration is σ1s2σ1s∗2σ2s2σ2s∗2π2px2=π2py2. The bond order is 8−42=2. Since the highest occupied molecular orbitals are both π orbitals, the double bond consists of two π bonds and no σ bond. Statement (C) is incorrect.

Statement (D): N2+ (13 electrons) has a bond order of 2.5 with 4 electrons in antibonding orbitals. N2− (15 electrons) also has a bond order of 2.5 but has 5 electrons in antibonding orbitals. Since N2− has more electrons in destabilizing antibonding orbitals, N2+ is structurally more stable. Statement (D) is correct.

Therefore, statements (A), (B), and (D) are correct.

Answer: (A), (B) and (D) only

(B) The wavelength corresponding to the series limit of the Paschen series in the He+ion is 4 times longer than that of the Paschen series in the Hydrogen atom.
(C) The n=4 to n=2 electronic transition in the He+ion emits a photon of identical wavelength to the first line of the Lyman series in the Hydrogen atom.
(D) The ratio of the shortest wavelength of the Lyman series in He+to the shortest wavelength of the Paschen series in the Hydrogen atom is 1:36.

Choose the most appropriate option from those given below:
questions.questionHtml:
Consider the following statements regarding the atomic spectra of hydrogen and hydrogen-like species:
(A) The ratio of the maximum wavelength to the minimum wavelength in the Balmer series of any hydrogen-like ion is always 9:5.
(B) The wavelength corresponding to the series limit of the Paschen series in the He+ion is 4 times longer than that of the Paschen series in the Hydrogen atom.
(C) The n=4 to n=2 electronic transition in the He+ion emits a photon of identical wavelength to the first line of the Lyman series in the Hydrogen atom.
(D) The ratio of the shortest wavelength of the Lyman series in He+to the shortest wavelength of the Paschen series in the Hydrogen atom is 1:36.

Choose the most appropriate option from those given below:
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement (A): For the Balmer series, n 1 = 2 . The maximum wavelength corresponds to the minimum energy transition ( n 2 = 3 → n 1 = 2 ): 1 λ m a x = R Z 2 ( 1 2 2 − 1 3 2 ) = R Z 2 ( 5 36 ) The minimum wavelength corresponds to the maximum energy transition ( n 2 = ∞ → n 1 = 2 ): 1 λ m i n = R Z 2 ( 1 2 2 − 1 ∞ 2 ) = R Z 2 ( 1 4 ) The ratio is λ m a x λ m i n = 1 / 4 5 / 36 = 9 5 . Statement (A) is correct. Statement (B): The series limit of the Paschen series corresponds to n 1 = 3 , n 2 = ∞ . 1 λ = R Z 2 ( 1 3 2 ) = R Z 2 9 For He + ( Z = 2 ), λ He + = 9 4 R . For H ( Z = 1 ), λ H = 9 R . Thus, λ He + = 1 4 λ H . The wavelength is 4 times shorter, not longer. Statement (B) is incorrect. Statement (C): For the n = 4 → n = 2 transition in He + ( Z = 2 ): 1 λ = R ( 2 2 ) ( 1 2 2 − 1 4 2 ) = 4 R ( 3 16 ) = 3 R 4 For the first line of the Lyman series in H ( Z = 1 , n = 2 → n = 1 ): 1 λ = R ( 1 2 ) ( 1 1 2 − 1 2 2 ) = 3 R 4 The wavelengths are identical. Statement (C) is correct. Statement (D): The shortest wavelength of the Lyman series in He + ( n 1 = 1 , n 2 = ∞ ): 1 λ 1 = R ( 2 2 ) ( 1 1 2 ) = 4 R ⇒ λ 1 = 1 4 R The shortest wavelength of the Paschen series in H ( n 1 = 3 , n 2 = ∞ ): 1 λ 2 = R ( 1 2 ) ( 1 3 2 ) = R 9 ⇒ λ 2 = 9 R The ratio is λ 1 λ 2 = 1 / ( 4 R ) 9 / R = 1 36 . Statement (D) is correct. Statements (A), (C), and (D) are correct. Answer: (A), (C), and (D) only are correct

The maximum wavelength corresponds to the minimum energy transition ( n 2 = 3 → n 1 = 2 ): 1 λ m a x = R Z 2 ( 1 2 2 − 1 3 2 ) = R Z 2 ( 5 36 ) The minimum wavelength corresponds to the maximum energy transition ( n 2 = ∞ → n 1 = 2 ): 1 λ m i n = R Z 2 ( 1 2 2 − 1 ∞ 2 ) = R Z 2 ( 1 4 ) The ratio is λ m a x λ m i n = 1 / 4 5 / 36 = 9 5 . Statement (A) is correct. Statement (B): The series limit of the Paschen series corresponds to n 1 = 3 , n 2 = ∞ . 1 λ = R Z 2 ( 1 3 2 ) = R Z 2 9 For He + ( Z = 2 ), λ He + = 9 4 R . For H ( Z = 1 ), λ H = 9 R . Thus, λ He + = 1 4 λ H . The wavelength is 4 times shorter, not longer. Statement (B) is incorrect. Statement (C): For the n = 4 → n = 2 transition in He + ( Z = 2 ): 1 λ = R ( 2 2 ) ( 1 2 2 − 1 4 2 ) = 4 R ( 3 16 ) = 3 R 4 For the first line of the Lyman series in H ( Z = 1 , n = 2 → n = 1 ): 1 λ = R ( 1 2 ) ( 1 1 2 − 1 2 2 ) = 3 R 4 The wavelengths are identical. Statement (C) is correct. Statement (D): The shortest wavelength of the Lyman series in He + ( n 1 = 1 , n 2 = ∞ ): 1 λ 1 = R ( 2 2 ) ( 1 1 2 ) = 4 R ⇒ λ 1 = 1 4 R The shortest wavelength of the Paschen series in H ( n 1 = 3 , n 2 = ∞ ): 1 λ 2 = R ( 1 2 ) ( 1 3 2 ) = R 9 ⇒ λ 2 = 9 R The ratio is λ 1 λ 2 = 1 / ( 4 R ) 9 / R = 1 36 . Statement (D) is correct. Statements (A), (C), and (D) are correct. Answer: (A), (C), and (D) only are correct
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Statement (A): For the Balmer series, n1=2.

The maximum wavelength corresponds to the minimum energy transition (n2=3→n1=2):
1λmax=RZ2(122−132)=RZ2(536)

The minimum wavelength corresponds to the maximum energy transition (n2=∞→n1=2):
1λmin=RZ2(122−1∞2)=RZ2(14)

The ratio is λmaxλmin=1/45/36=95. Statement (A) is correct.

Statement (B): The series limit of the Paschen series corresponds to n1=3,n2=∞.
1λ=RZ2(132)=RZ29

For He+ (Z=2), λHe+=94R. For H (Z=1), λH=9R.
Thus, λHe+=14λH. The wavelength is 4 times shorter, not longer. Statement (B) is incorrect.

Statement (C): For the n=4→n=2 transition in He+ (Z=2):
1λ=R(22)(122−142)=4R(316)=3R4

For the first line of the Lyman series in H (Z=1, n=2→n=1):
1λ=R(12)(112−122)=3R4

The wavelengths are identical. Statement (C) is correct.

Statement (D): The shortest wavelength of the Lyman series in He+ (n1=1,n2=∞):
1λ1=R(22)(112)=4R⇒λ1=14R

The shortest wavelength of the Paschen series in H (n1=3,n2=∞):
1λ2=R(12)(132)=R9⇒λ2=9R

The ratio is λ1λ2=1/(4R)9/R=136. Statement (D) is correct.

Statements (A), (C), and (D) are correct.

Answer: (A), (C), and (D) only are correct

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Liquids A and B form an ideal solution at a constant temperature. The vapour pressures of pure A and pure B are 400 Torr and 500 Torr, respectively. Let X A and X B be their mole fractions in the liquid phase, while Y A and Y B are their mole fractions in the vapour phase. If a graph is plotted with ( Y A Y B ) on the y -axis against ( X A X B ) on the x -axis, which statement correctly describes the resulting plot?
questions.questionText: Liquids A and B form an ideal solution at a constant temperature. The vapour pressures of pure A and pure B are 400 Torr and 500 Torr, respectively. Let X A and X B be their mole fractions in the liquid phase, while Y A and Y B are their mole fractions in the vapour phase. If a graph is plotted with ( Y A Y B ) on the y -axis against ( X A X B ) on the x -axis, which statement correctly describes the resulting plot?

Let X A and X B be their mole fractions in the liquid phase, while Y A and Y B are their mole fractions in the vapour phase. questions.html: Liquids A and B form an ideal solution at a constant temperature. The vapour pressures of pure A and pure B are 400 Torr and 500 Torr, respectively. Let XA and XB be their mole fractions in the liquid phase, while YA and YB are their mole fractions in the vapour phase. If a graph is plotted with (YAYB) on the y-axis against (XAXB) on the x -axis, which statement correctly describes the resulting plot?
questions.questionHtml: Liquids A and B form an ideal solution at a constant temperature. The vapour pressures of pure A and pure B are 400 Torr and 500 Torr, respectively. Let XA and XB be their mole fractions in the liquid phase, while YA and YB are their mole fractions in the vapour phase. If a graph is plotted with (YAYB) on the y-axis against (XAXB) on the x -axis, which statement correctly describes the resulting plot?
questions.solutionText: Solution According to Raoult's law, the partial vapour pressures of components A and B are given by P A = P A 0 X A and P B = P B 0 X B . According to Dalton's law of partial pressures, the mole fractions in the vapour phase are Y A = P A P t o t a l and Y B = P B P t o t a l . Taking the ratio of Y A to Y B , we get: Y A Y B = P A P B = P A 0 X A P B 0 X B Substituting the given values P A 0 = 400 Torr and P B 0 = 500 Torr: Y A Y B = 400 500 ( X A X B ) = 0.8 ( X A X B ) Comparing this with the equation of a straight line y = m x + c , we get y = Y A Y B and x = X A X B . The equation represents a straight line passing through the origin ( c = 0 ) with a slope m = 0.8 . Answer: A straight line passing through the origin with a slope of 0.8.
questions.solution: Solution According to Raoult's law, the partial vapour pressures of components A and B are given by P A = P A 0 X A and P B = P B 0 X B . According to Dalton's law of partial pressures, the mole fractions in the vapour phase are Y A = P A P t o t a l and Y B = P B P t o t a l . Taking the ratio of Y A to Y B , we get: Y A Y B = P A P B = P A 0 X A P B 0 X B Substituting the given values P A 0 = 400 Torr and P B 0 = 500 Torr: Y A Y B = 400 500 ( X A X B ) = 0.8 ( X A X B ) Comparing this with the equation of a straight line y = m x + c , we get y = Y A Y B and x = X A X B . The equation represents a straight line passing through the origin ( c = 0 ) with a slope m = 0.8 . Answer: A straight line passing through the origin with a slope of 0.8.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
According to Raoult's law, the partial vapour pressures of components A and B are given by PA=PA0XA and PB=PB0XB.

According to Dalton's law of partial pressures, the mole fractions in the vapour phase are YA=PAPtotal and YB=PBPtotal.

Taking the ratio of YA to YB, we get:

YAYB=PAPB=PA0XAPB0XB

Substituting the given values PA0=400 Torr and PB0=500 Torr:

YAYB=400500(XAXB)=0.8(XAXB)

Comparing this with the equation of a straight line y=mx+c, we get y=YAYB and x=XAXB.

The equation represents a straight line passing through the origin (c=0) with a slope m=0.8.

Answer: A straight line passing through the origin with a slope of 0.8.

questions.text: The major product of the following reaction is: → (i) NaBH 4 , EtOH (ii) H 3 O + , Δ
questions.questionText: The major product of the following reaction is: → (i) NaBH 4 , EtOH (ii) H 3 O + , Δ
questions.question: The major product of the following reaction is: → (i) NaBH 4 , EtOH (ii) H 3 O + , Δ
questions.prompt: The major product of the following reaction is: → (i) NaBH 4 , EtOH (ii) H 3 O + , Δ
questions.html:
The major product of the following reaction is:

→ (i) NaBH4,EtOH (ii) H3O+,Δ
questions.questionHtml:
The major product of the following reaction is:

→ (i) NaBH4,EtOH (ii) H3O+,Δ
questions.solutionText: Solution The first step involves the reaction with N a B H 4 in ethanol. Sodium borohydride is a chemoselective reducing agent that reduces the aldehyde group ( − C H O ) to a primary alcohol ( − C H 2 O H ) but leaves the nitrile group ( − C N ) unaffected. The intermediate formed is 2-(cyanomethyl)benzyl alcohol. The second step is heating the intermediate with aqueous acid ( H 3 O + ). This hydrolyzes the nitrile group to a carboxylic acid ( − C O O H ), producing 2-(hydroxymethyl)phenylacetic acid. This molecule contains both a hydroxyl group and a carboxylic acid group in close proximity. Under the acidic and heated conditions, it undergoes intramolecular esterification (lactonization) to form a stable six-membered ring by the loss of a water molecule. The resulting major product is a lactone where the oxygen atom links the − C H 2 − group of the former alcohol to the carbonyl carbon of the former acetic acid group. This corresponds to the structure shown in option (4).
questions.solution: Solution The first step involves the reaction with N a B H 4 in ethanol. Sodium borohydride is a chemoselective reducing agent that reduces the aldehyde group ( − C H O ) to a primary alcohol ( − C H 2 O H ) but leaves the nitrile group ( − C N ) unaffected. The intermediate formed is 2-(cyanomethyl)benzyl alcohol. The second step is heating the intermediate with aqueous acid ( H 3 O + ). This hydrolyzes the nitrile group to a carboxylic acid ( − C O O H ), producing 2-(hydroxymethyl)phenylacetic acid. This molecule contains both a hydroxyl group and a carboxylic acid group in close proximity. Under the acidic and heated conditions, it undergoes intramolecular esterification (lactonization) to form a stable six-membered ring by the loss of a water molecule. The resulting major product is a lactone where the oxygen atom links the − C H 2 − group of the former alcohol to the carbonyl carbon of the former acetic acid group. This corresponds to the structure shown in option (4).
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The first step involves the reaction with NaBH4 in ethanol. Sodium borohydride is a chemoselective reducing agent that reduces the aldehyde group (−CHO) to a primary alcohol (−CH2OH) but leaves the nitrile group (−CN) unaffected. The intermediate formed is 2-(cyanomethyl)benzyl alcohol.

The second step is heating the intermediate with aqueous acid (H3O+). This hydrolyzes the nitrile group to a carboxylic acid (−COOH), producing 2-(hydroxymethyl)phenylacetic acid.

This molecule contains both a hydroxyl group and a carboxylic acid group in close proximity. Under the acidic and heated conditions, it undergoes intramolecular esterification (lactonization) to form a stable six-membered ring by the loss of a water molecule.

The resulting major product is a lactone where the oxygen atom links the −CH2− group of the former alcohol to the carbonyl carbon of the former acetic acid group. This corresponds to the structure shown in option (4).

questions.questionHtml:
Given below are five statements regarding metallurgical processes:
(A) Pine oil acts as a froth stabilizer.
(B) Siderite is a carbonate ore of iron.
(C) Copper matte contains Cu2 S and FeS .
(D) Zone refining is used for impure titanium.
(E) Cryolite lowers the melting point of alumina.

Choose the most appropriate answer from the options given below:
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement (A) is incorrect because pine oil acts as a frothing agent, whereas aniline and cresols act as froth stabilizers. Statement (B) is correct because siderite is FeCO 3 , which is a carbonate ore of iron. Statement (C) is correct because copper matte is primarily a mixture of Cu 2 S and FeS . Statement (D) is incorrect because zone refining is used for producing semiconductors of very high purity like silicon, germanium, boron, gallium, and indium. Titanium is refined by the van Arkel method. Statement (E) is correct because cryolite ( Na 3 AlF 6 ) is added to alumina to lower its melting point and increase the electrical conductivity of the mixture. Thus, statements (B), (C), and (E) are correct. Answer: (B), (C) and (E) only

Statement (D) is incorrect because zone refining is used for producing semiconductors of very high purity like silicon, germanium, boron, gallium, and indium. Titanium is refined by the van Arkel method. Statement (E) is correct because cryolite ( Na 3 AlF 6 ) is added to alumina to lower its melting point and increase the electrical conductivity of the mixture. Thus, statements (B), (C), and (E) are correct. Answer: (B), (C) and (E) only

Solution
Statement (A) is incorrect because pine oil acts as a frothing agent, whereas aniline and cresols act as froth stabilizers.

Statement (B) is correct because siderite is FeCO3, which is a carbonate ore of iron.

Statement (C) is correct because copper matte is primarily a mixture of Cu2S and FeS.

Statement (D) is incorrect because zone refining is used for producing semiconductors of very high purity like silicon, germanium, boron, gallium, and indium. Titanium is refined by the van Arkel method.

Statement (E) is correct because cryolite (Na3AlF6) is added to alumina to lower its melting point and increase the electrical conductivity of the mixture.

Thus, statements (B), (C), and (E) are correct.

Answer: (B), (C) and (E) only

questions.solutionText: Solution The percentage of carbon in the organic compound is given by: % C = 12 44 × Mass of CO 2 Mass of compound × 100 % C = 12 44 × 0.15 0.2 × 100 = 20.45 % ≈ 20 % The percentage of hydrogen in the organic compound is given by: % H = 2 18 × Mass of H 2 O Mass of compound × 100 % H = 2 18 × 0.12 0.2 × 100 = 6.66 % The percentage of oxygen is calculated by subtracting the percentages of carbon and hydrogen from 100 % : % O = 100 − ( % C + % H ) % O = 100 − ( 20 + 6.66 ) = 73.34 % Answer: C = 20 % , H = 6.66 % , O = 73.34 %
questions.solution: Solution The percentage of carbon in the organic compound is given by: % C = 12 44 × Mass of CO 2 Mass of compound × 100 % C = 12 44 × 0.15 0.2 × 100 = 20.45 % ≈ 20 % The percentage of hydrogen in the organic compound is given by: % H = 2 18 × Mass of H 2 O Mass of compound × 100 % H = 2 18 × 0.12 0.2 × 100 = 6.66 % The percentage of oxygen is calculated by subtracting the percentages of carbon and hydrogen from 100 % : % O = 100 − ( % C + % H ) % O = 100 − ( 20 + 6.66 ) = 73.34 % Answer: C = 20 % , H = 6.66 % , O = 73.34 %
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The percentage of carbon in the organic compound is given by:

%C=1244×Mass of CO2Mass of compound×100

%C=1244×0.150.2×100=20.45%≈20%

The percentage of hydrogen in the organic compound is given by:

%H=218×Mass of H2OMass of compound×100

%H=218×0.120.2×100=6.66%

The percentage of oxygen is calculated by subtracting the percentages of carbon and hydrogen from 100%:

%O=100−(%C+%H)

%O=100−(20+6.66)=73.34%

Answer: C = 20%, H = 6.66%, O = 73.34%

Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies. IV. Noble gases exhibit positive electron gain enthalpies. V. Screening effect decreases moving down a group. Choose the correct statements from the options below:

Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies. IV. Noble gases exhibit positive electron gain enthalpies. V. Screening effect decreases moving down a group. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies. IV. Noble gases exhibit positive electron gain enthalpies. V. Screening effect decreases moving down a group. questions.html:
Assess the following periodic trend statements:
I. Atomic radius decreases from left to right.
II. Electronegativity increases moving down a specific group.
III. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies.
IV. Noble gases exhibit positive electron gain enthalpies.
V. Screening effect decreases moving down a group.

Choose the correct statements from the options below:
questions.questionHtml:
Assess the following periodic trend statements:
I. Atomic radius decreases from left to right.
II. Electronegativity increases moving down a specific group.
III. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies.
IV. Noble gases exhibit positive electron gain enthalpies.
V. Screening effect decreases moving down a group.

Choose the correct statements from the options below:
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement I is correct. Atomic radius generally decreases from left to right across a period due to an increase in effective nuclear charge, which pulls the electron cloud closer to the nucleus. Statement II is incorrect. Electronegativity generally decreases down a group because atomic size increases, making the nucleus less effective at attracting shared electrons. Statement III is correct. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies because they require only one electron to achieve a stable noble gas electron configuration. Statement IV is correct. Noble gases have completely filled valence shells, so adding an electron requires energy to place it in a higher energy level, resulting in a positive electron gain enthalpy. Statement V is incorrect. The screening (or shielding) effect increases down a group due to the addition of new inner electron shells. Therefore, statements I, III, and IV are correct. Answer: I, III, and IV only
questions.solution: Solution Statement I is correct. Atomic radius generally decreases from left to right across a period due to an increase in effective nuclear charge, which pulls the electron cloud closer to the nucleus. Statement II is incorrect. Electronegativity generally decreases down a group because atomic size increases, making the nucleus less effective at attracting shared electrons. Statement III is correct. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies because they require only one electron to achieve a stable noble gas electron configuration. Statement IV is correct. Noble gases have completely filled valence shells, so adding an electron requires energy to place it in a higher energy level, resulting in a positive electron gain enthalpy. Statement V is incorrect. The screening (or shielding) effect increases down a group due to the addition of new inner electron shells. Therefore, statements I, III, and IV are correct. Answer: I, III, and IV only
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Statement I is correct. Atomic radius generally decreases from left to right across a period due to an increase in effective nuclear charge, which pulls the electron cloud closer to the nucleus.

Statement II is incorrect. Electronegativity generally decreases down a group because atomic size increases, making the nucleus less effective at attracting shared electrons.

Statement III is correct. Halogens have highly negative electron gain enthalpies because they require only one electron to achieve a stable noble gas electron configuration.

Statement IV is correct. Noble gases have completely filled valence shells, so adding an electron requires energy to place it in a higher energy level, resulting in a positive electron gain enthalpy.

Statement V is incorrect. The screening (or shielding) effect increases down a group due to the addition of new inner electron shells.

Therefore, statements I, III, and IV are correct.

Answer: I, III, and IV only

Choose the correct statements from the options below:
questions.questionHtml:
Consider the following statements regarding the chemistry of Group 13 elements:
(A) Thallium(III) chloride acts as a strong oxidizing agent.
(B) The stability of the +1 oxidation state follows the sequence Al<Ga<In<TI.
(C) Orthoboric acid behaves as a classical proton-donating acid in aqueous solutions.
(D) The Lewis acid character of boron trihalides follows the order BF3>BCl3>BBr3.
(E) Aluminium is rendered passive by concentrated nitric acid.

Choose the correct statements from the options below:
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement (A) is correct because the + 1 oxidation state of thallium is more stable than the + 3 state due to the inert pair effect, making Tl 3 + a strong oxidizing agent. Statement (B) is correct as the inert pair effect becomes more prominent down the group, increasing the stability of the + 1 oxidation state in the order Al < Ga < In < Tl . Statement (C) is incorrect because orthoboric acid ( H 3 BO 3 ) is a weak monobasic Lewis acid. It does not donate a proton directly but accepts an OH − ion from water, releasing an H + ion. Statement (D) is incorrect because the Lewis acid strength of boron trihalides follows the order BF 3 < BCl 3 < BBr 3 < BI 3 . This is due to the decreasing strength of p π − p π back-bonding from the halogen to the empty 2 p orbital of boron as the size of the halogen increases. Statement (E) is correct because concentrated nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent that forms a protective oxide layer ( Al 2 O 3 ) on the surface of aluminium, rendering it passive. Thus, statements (A), (B), and (E) are correct. Answer: A), (B) and (E) only

Statement (E) is correct because concentrated nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent that forms a protective oxide layer ( Al 2 O 3 ) on the surface of aluminium, rendering it passive. Statement (C) is incorrect because orthoboric acid (H3BO3) is a weak monobasic Lewis acid. It does not donate a proton directly but accepts an OH− ion from water, releasing an H+ ion.

Statement (D) is incorrect because the Lewis acid strength of boron trihalides follows the order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<BI3. This is due to the decreasing strength of pπ−pπ back-bonding from the halogen to the empty 2p orbital of boron as the size of the halogen increases.

Statement (E) is correct because concentrated nitric acid is a strong oxidizing agent that forms a protective oxide layer (Al2O3) on the surface of aluminium, rendering it passive.

Answer: A), (B) and (E) only

questions.text: Compound A ( C 10 H 12 O ) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I 2 / NaOH , but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C 8 H 6 O 4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.questionText: Compound A ( C 10 H 12 O ) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I 2 / NaOH , but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C 8 H 6 O 4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.question: Compound A ( C 10 H 12 O ) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I 2 / NaOH , but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C 8 H 6 O 4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.prompt: Compound A ( C 10 H 12 O ) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I 2 / NaOH , but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C 8 H 6 O 4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.html: Compound A (C10H12O) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I2/NaOH, but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C8H6O4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.questionHtml: Compound A (C10H12O) is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent and I2/NaOH, but gives a 2,4-DNP derivative. Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C8H6O4 ), a monomer for Terylene. Compound A is:
questions.solutionText: Solution Compound A gives a 2,4-DNP derivative, indicating the presence of a carbonyl group (aldehyde or ketone). It is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent, which means it is not an aldehyde. Therefore, A must be a ketone. It is unreactive towards I 2 / NaOH (iodoform test), which means it does not contain a methyl ketone group ( -CO-CH 3 ). Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B ( C 8 H 6 O 4 ), which is a monomer for Terylene. Terylene is a polyester formed from ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid (benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid). Thus, acid B is terephthalic acid, indicating that compound A is a para-disubstituted benzene derivative. Evaluating the given options: Option (1): 1-(4-ethylphenyl)ethan-1-one contains a -CO-CH 3 group and would give a positive iodoform test. Option (2): 1-(4-methylphenyl)propan-1-one is a ketone (Tollens' negative), lacks a -CO-CH 3 group (iodoform negative), and is para-disubstituted. Vigorous oxidation of the para-alkyl groups ( -CH 3 and -CO-CH 2 CH 3 ) yields terephthalic acid. Option (3): 1-(2-methylphenyl)propan-1-one is ortho-disubstituted and would yield phthalic acid upon oxidation. Option (4): 4-propylbenzaldehyde is an aldehyde and would give a positive Tollens' test. Therefore, compound A is 1-(4-methylphenyl)propan-1-one. Answer:

Vigorous oxidation of the para-alkyl groups ( -CH 3 and -CO-CH 2 CH 3 ) yields terephthalic acid. Option (3): 1-(2-methylphenyl)propan-1-one is ortho-disubstituted and would yield phthalic acid upon oxidation. Option (4): 4-propylbenzaldehyde is an aldehyde and would give a positive Tollens' test. Therefore, compound A is 1-(4-methylphenyl)propan-1-one. Answer:
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Compound A gives a 2,4-DNP derivative, indicating the presence of a carbonyl group (aldehyde or ketone).

It is unreactive towards Tollens' reagent, which means it is not an aldehyde. Therefore, A must be a ketone.

It is unreactive towards I2/NaOH (iodoform test), which means it does not contain a methyl ketone group (-CO-CH3).

Vigorous oxidation of A yields acid B (C8H6O4), which is a monomer for Terylene. Terylene is a polyester formed from ethylene glycol and terephthalic acid (benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid). Thus, acid B is terephthalic acid, indicating that compound A is a para-disubstituted benzene derivative.

Evaluating the given options:
Option (1): 1-(4-ethylphenyl)ethan-1-one contains a -CO-CH3 group and would give a positive iodoform test.
Option (2): 1-(4-methylphenyl)propan-1-one is a ketone (Tollens' negative), lacks a -CO-CH3 group (iodoform negative), and is para-disubstituted. Vigorous oxidation of the para-alkyl groups (-CH3 and -CO-CH2CH3) yields terephthalic acid.
Option (3): 1-(2-methylphenyl)propan-1-one is ortho-disubstituted and would yield phthalic acid upon oxidation.
Option (4): 4-propylbenzaldehyde is an aldehyde and would give a positive Tollens' test.

Therefore, compound A is 1-(4-methylphenyl)propan-1-one.

Answer:

The equilibrium constant for the oxidation of 2 mole NH3 by oxygen to give NO is
questions.questionHtml:
The following equilibrium constants are given:
N2+3H2⇌2NH3;K1
N2+O2⇌2NO;K2
H2+1/2O2⇌H2O;K3
The equilibrium constant for the oxidation of 2 mole NH3 by oxygen to give NO is
questions.solutionText: Solution The given reactions are: (i) N 2 + 3 H 2 ⇌ 2 NH 3 K 1 (ii) N 2 + O 2 ⇌ 2 NO K 2 (iii) H 2 + 1 2 O 2 ⇌ H 2 O K 3 The target reaction for the oxidation of 2 moles of NH 3 to NO is: 2 NH 3 + 5 2 O 2 ⇌ 2 NO + 3 H 2 O This reaction can be obtained by reversing reaction (i), adding reaction (ii), and adding 3 times reaction (iii): 2 NH 3 ⇌ N 2 + 3 H 2 K = 1 K 1 N 2 + O 2 ⇌ 2 NO K = K 2 3 H 2 + 3 2 O 2 ⇌ 3 H 2 O K = K 3 3 Adding these three reactions gives the target reaction. The equilibrium constant for the target reaction is the product of the individual equilibrium constants: K e q = 1 K 1 × K 2 × K 3 3 = K 2 K 3 3 K 1 Answer: K 2 K 3 3 K 1
questions.solution: Solution The given reactions are: (i) N 2 + 3 H 2 ⇌ 2 NH 3 K 1 (ii) N 2 + O 2 ⇌ 2 NO K 2 (iii) H 2 + 1 2 O 2 ⇌ H 2 O K 3 The target reaction for the oxidation of 2 moles of NH 3 to NO is: 2 NH 3 + 5 2 O 2 ⇌ 2 NO + 3 H 2 O This reaction can be obtained by reversing reaction (i), adding reaction (ii), and adding 3 times reaction (iii): 2 NH 3 ⇌ N 2 + 3 H 2 K = 1 K 1 N 2 + O 2 ⇌ 2 NO K = K 2 3 H 2 + 3 2 O 2 ⇌ 3 H 2 O K = K 3 3 Adding these three reactions gives the target reaction. The equilibrium constant for the target reaction is the product of the individual equilibrium constants: K e q = 1 K 1 × K 2 × K 3 3 = K 2 K 3 3 K 1 Answer: K 2 K 3 3 K 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The given reactions are:
(i) N2+3H2⇌2NH3K1
(ii) N2+O2⇌2NOK2
(iii) H2+12O2⇌H2OK3

The target reaction for the oxidation of 2 moles of NH3 to NO is:
2NH3+52O2⇌2NO+3H2O

This reaction can be obtained by reversing reaction (i), adding reaction (ii), and adding 3 times reaction (iii):
2NH3⇌N2+3H2K=1K1
N2+O2⇌2NOK=K2
3H2+32O2⇌3H2OK=K33

Adding these three reactions gives the target reaction. The equilibrium constant for the target reaction is the product of the individual equilibrium constants:
Keq=1K1×K2×K33=K2K33K1

Answer: K2K33K1

questions.text: 4.5 g of aluminium (at mass 27 amu) is deposited at cathode from Al 3 + solution by a certain quantity of electric charge. The volume of hydrogen produced at STP from H + ions in solution by the same quantity of electric charge will be

questions.questionHtml: 4.5 g of aluminium (at mass 27 amu) is deposited at cathode from Al3+ solution by a certain quantity of electric charge. The volume of hydrogen produced at STP from H+ ions in solution by the same quantity of electric charge will be
questions.solutionText: Solution Reaction for the deposition of aluminium: Al 3 + + 3 e − → Al Moles of aluminium deposited = 4.5 27 = 1 6 mol Charge passed = 3 × 1 6 = 0.5 F Reaction for the liberation of hydrogen gas: 2 H + + 2 e − → H 2 Moles of H 2 produced by 0.5 F charge = 0.5 2 = 0.25 mol Volume of H 2 gas at STP = 0.25 × 22.4 L = 5.6 L Answer: 5.6 L

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Reaction for the deposition of aluminium:

Al3++3e−→Al

Moles of aluminium deposited = 4.527=16 mol

Charge passed = 3×16=0.5 F

Reaction for the liberation of hydrogen gas:

2H++2e−→H2

Moles of H2 produced by 0.5 F charge = 0.52=0.25 mol

Volume of H2 gas at STP = 0.25×22.4 L=5.6 L

Answer: 5.6 L

questions.solutionText: Solution Hexan-1,6-dioic acid (adipic acid) and Hexane-1,6-diamine (hexamethylenediamine) undergo condensation polymerization to form Nylon-6,6. It is a step-growth polymer (polyamide) characterized by strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding. Thus, (A) → (III). 2-Chlorobuta-1,3-diene (chloroprene) undergoes addition polymerization to form Neoprene. It is an addition homopolymer and a synthetic elastomer. Thus, (B) → (IV). Benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid (terephthalic acid) and Ethane-1,2-diol (ethylene glycol) undergo condensation polymerization to form Terylene (Dacron). It is a step-growth polymer (polyester) held together by strong dipole-dipole interactions. Thus, (C) → (I). 2-Methylbuta-1,3-diene (isoprene) undergoes addition polymerization to form polyisoprene (natural rubber). It is an addition homopolymer and a natural elastomer. Thus, (D) → (II). Answer: (A) → (III), (B) → (IV), (C) → (I), (D) → (II)

Benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid (terephthalic acid) and Ethane-1,2-diol (ethylene glycol) undergo condensation polymerization to form Terylene (Dacron). Thus, (C) → (I). 2-Methylbuta-1,3-diene (isoprene) undergoes addition polymerization to form polyisoprene (natural rubber). It is an addition homopolymer and a natural elastomer. Thus, (D) → (II). Answer: (A) → (III), (B) → (IV), (C) → (I), (D) → (II)
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Hexan-1,6-dioic acid (adipic acid) and Hexane-1,6-diamine (hexamethylenediamine) undergo condensation polymerization to form Nylon-6,6. It is a step-growth polymer (polyamide) characterized by strong intermolecular hydrogen bonding. Thus, (A) → (III).

2-Chlorobuta-1,3-diene (chloroprene) undergoes addition polymerization to form Neoprene. It is an addition homopolymer and a synthetic elastomer. Thus, (B) → (IV).

Benzene-1,4-dicarboxylic acid (terephthalic acid) and Ethane-1,2-diol (ethylene glycol) undergo condensation polymerization to form Terylene (Dacron). It is a step-growth polymer (polyester) held together by strong dipole-dipole interactions. Thus, (C) → (I).

2-Methylbuta-1,3-diene (isoprene) undergoes addition polymerization to form polyisoprene (natural rubber). It is an addition homopolymer and a natural elastomer. Thus, (D) → (II).

Answer: (A) → (III), (B) → (IV), (C) → (I), (D) → (II)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.text: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.questionText: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.question: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.prompt: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.html: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.questionHtml: Which of the following is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene?
questions.solutionText: Solution Reduction of nitrobenzene with Sn and concentrated HCl followed by NaOH ( a q ) yields benzenamine (aniline). Catalytic hydrogenation of nitrobenzene using H 2 gas in the presence of finely divided Pd in ethanol also produces benzenamine. Reduction of nitrobenzene with Fe scrap and HCl followed by NaOH ( a q ) is the preferred industrial method for the preparation of benzenamine. However, reduction of nitrobenzene with LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether does not produce benzenamine. Instead, it yields azobenzene as the major product. Therefore, LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether followed by H 3 O + is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene. Answer: LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether followed by H 3 O +
questions.solution: Solution Reduction of nitrobenzene with Sn and concentrated HCl followed by NaOH ( a q ) yields benzenamine (aniline). Catalytic hydrogenation of nitrobenzene using H 2 gas in the presence of finely divided Pd in ethanol also produces benzenamine. Reduction of nitrobenzene with Fe scrap and HCl followed by NaOH ( a q ) is the preferred industrial method for the preparation of benzenamine. However, reduction of nitrobenzene with LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether does not produce benzenamine. Instead, it yields azobenzene as the major product. Therefore, LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether followed by H 3 O + is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene. Answer: LiAlH 4 in anhydrous ether followed by H 3 O +
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Reduction of nitrobenzene with Sn and concentrated HCl followed by NaOH(aq) yields benzenamine (aniline).

Catalytic hydrogenation of nitrobenzene using H2 gas in the presence of finely divided Pd in ethanol also produces benzenamine.

Reduction of nitrobenzene with Fe scrap and HCl followed by NaOH(aq) is the preferred industrial method for the preparation of benzenamine.

However, reduction of nitrobenzene with LiAlH4 in anhydrous ether does not produce benzenamine. Instead, it yields azobenzene as the major product.

Therefore, LiAlH4 in anhydrous ether followed by H3O+ is not a suitable method for the preparation of benzenamine from nitrobenzene.

Answer: LiAlH4 in anhydrous ether followed by H3O+

questions.text: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.questionText: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.question: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.prompt: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.html: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.questionHtml: Strongest nucleophile is
questions.solutionText: Solution Nucleophilicity depends on the availability of electrons to be donated to an electrophile. Negatively charged species are generally stronger nucleophiles than neutral species. Thus, CH 3 O − and C 6 H 5 O − are stronger nucleophiles than RNH 2 and ROH . Between CH 3 O − and C 6 H 5 O − , the negative charge on the phenoxide ion ( C 6 H 5 O − ) is delocalized over the benzene ring due to resonance, which decreases its electron-donating ability. In the methoxide ion ( CH 3 O − ), the negative charge is localized on the oxygen atom, and the +I effect of the methyl group further increases the electron density on oxygen. Therefore, CH 3 O − is the strongest nucleophile. Answer: CH 3 O −
questions.solution: Solution Nucleophilicity depends on the availability of electrons to be donated to an electrophile. Negatively charged species are generally stronger nucleophiles than neutral species. Thus, CH 3 O − and C 6 H 5 O − are stronger nucleophiles than RNH 2 and ROH . Between CH 3 O − and C 6 H 5 O − , the negative charge on the phenoxide ion ( C 6 H 5 O − ) is delocalized over the benzene ring due to resonance, which decreases its electron-donating ability. In the methoxide ion ( CH 3 O − ), the negative charge is localized on the oxygen atom, and the +I effect of the methyl group further increases the electron density on oxygen. Therefore, CH 3 O − is the strongest nucleophile. Answer: CH 3 O −
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Nucleophilicity depends on the availability of electrons to be donated to an electrophile.

Negatively charged species are generally stronger nucleophiles than neutral species. Thus, CH3O− and C6H5O− are stronger nucleophiles than RNH2 and ROH.

Between CH3O− and C6H5O−, the negative charge on the phenoxide ion (C6H5O−) is delocalized over the benzene ring due to resonance, which decreases its electron-donating ability.

In the methoxide ion (CH3O−), the negative charge is localized on the oxygen atom, and the +I effect of the methyl group further increases the electron density on oxygen.

Therefore, CH3O− is the strongest nucleophile.

Answer: CH3O−

questions.questionHtml:
Pick out the incorrect statement(s) from the following:
1. All naturally occurring α-amino acids are optically active except glycine.
2. In aqueous solutions, amino acids exist as dipolar ions known as zwitterions.
3. Proteins are polymers of α-amino acids connected to each other by glycosidic linkages.
4. The α-helix and β-pleated sheet are examples of the secondary structure of proteins, stabilized by hydrogen bonds.
5. During the denaturation of a protein, its primary, secondary, and tertiary structures are all completely destroyed.
questions.solutionText: Solution Statement 1 is correct. Glycine has two hydrogen atoms on the α -carbon, making it achiral and optically inactive, while all other naturally occurring α -amino acids are chiral. Statement 2 is correct. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group loses a proton and the amino group gains a proton, forming a dipolar ion known as a zwitterion. Statement 3 is incorrect. Proteins are polymers of α -amino acids connected by peptide linkages, whereas glycosidic linkages are found in carbohydrates. Statement 4 is correct. The α -helix and β -pleated sheet are secondary structures stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the − N H − and − C = O groups of the peptide bond. Statement 5 is incorrect. During denaturation, the secondary and tertiary structures of proteins are destroyed, but the primary structure remains intact. Therefore, statements 3 and 5 are incorrect. Answer: 3 and 5 only
questions.solution: Solution Statement 1 is correct. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group loses a proton and the amino group gains a proton, forming a dipolar ion known as a zwitterion. Statement 3 is incorrect. Proteins are polymers of α -amino acids connected by peptide linkages, whereas glycosidic linkages are found in carbohydrates. Statement 4 is correct. The α -helix and β -pleated sheet are secondary structures stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the − N H − and − C = O groups of the peptide bond. Statement 5 is incorrect. During denaturation, the secondary and tertiary structures of proteins are destroyed, but the primary structure remains intact. Therefore, statements 3 and 5 are incorrect. Answer: 3 and 5 only
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Statement 1 is correct. Glycine has two hydrogen atoms on the α-carbon, making it achiral and optically inactive, while all other naturally occurring α-amino acids are chiral.

Statement 2 is correct. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group loses a proton and the amino group gains a proton, forming a dipolar ion known as a zwitterion.

Statement 3 is incorrect. Proteins are polymers of α-amino acids connected by peptide linkages, whereas glycosidic linkages are found in carbohydrates.

Statement 4 is correct. The α-helix and β-pleated sheet are secondary structures stabilized by hydrogen bonds between the −NH− and −C=O groups of the peptide bond.

Statement 5 is incorrect. During denaturation, the secondary and tertiary structures of proteins are destroyed, but the primary structure remains intact.

Therefore, statements 3 and 5 are incorrect.

Answer: 3 and 5 only
